content:
### Task 1.1: Extend `EntryMetadata` with new fields

Run: `cd /workspaces/corvia-workspace/repos/corvia && cargo test -p corvia-common test_entry_metadata_new_fields -- --nocapture`
Expected: FAIL — `content_role` and `source_origin` fields don't exist on `EntryMetadata`

- [ ] **Step 3: Add fields to `EntryMetadata`**

In `types.rs`, add to the `EntryMetadata` struct after `end_line`:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Default)]
pub struct EntryMetadata {
    pub source_file: Option<String>,
    pub language: Option<String>,
    pub chunk_type: Option<String>,
    pub start_line: Option<u32>,
    pub end_line: Option<u32>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub content_role: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub source_origin: Option<String>,
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run test to verify it passes**

Run: `cd /workspaces/corvia-workspace/repos/corvia && cargo test -p corvia-common test_entry_metadata_new_fields -- --nocapture`
Expected: PASS

- [ ] **Step 5: Run full corvia-common tests to verify no regressions**

Run: `cd /workspaces/corvia-workspace/repos/corvia && cargo test -p corvia-common`
Expected: All tests pass. Existing serialization tests still work because `#[serde(default)]` makes new fields optional.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
cd /workspaces/corvia-workspace/repos/corvia
git add crates/corvia-common/src/types.rs
git commit -m "feat(common): add content_role and source_origin to EntryMetadata"
```

---
